qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Symlink-based path traversal in package tarball extraction allows arbitrary file write
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes path traversal via an untrusted version string passed to downloadPackageManager() where ../ segments escape VP_HOME. This finding describes a different root cause: symlink-based path traversal in tar::Archive::unpack() during tarball extraction. The vulnerable code locations differ (crates/vite_install/src/request.rs vs the advisory's vite-plus/binding downloadPackageManager()), the missing security check differs (symlink validation vs version string validation), and the attack mechanism differs (malicious symlink in tarball content vs ../ in version path component).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-33r3-4whc-44c2) describes path traversal in downloadPackageManager() in vite-plus/binding where an untrusted version string containing ../ segments escapes the VP_HOME/package_manager/<pm>/ cache root. The only qualifying finding ('tarball-extraction-path-traversal') describes a different path traversal mechanism: symlink-based traversal in tar::Archive::unpack() during tarball extraction, with different vulnerable code locations (crates/vite_install/src/request.rs vs the advisory's vite-plus/binding), different missing security checks (symlink validation vs version string validation), and a different attack vector. No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
